walkthrough.title: Assemble the Train
walkthrough.text: Howdy, partner! Before your first game, you need to build the 3D train. Pop out the cardboard pieces and assemble the six Train Cars and the Locomotive using the enclosed instructions. Once built, they stay assembled for future games. For this session, grab the Locomotive and as many Cars as there are players in your posse. Line them up in a row in any order you like, with the Locomotive at the front. This is the Union Pacific Express, and you bandits are about to rob it blind.

walkthrough.title: Load the Loot
walkthrough.text: Time to stock the train with valuables. Each Car has loot icons printed on its floor showing what goes inside. Place the indicated Purse tokens (worth $250 to $500 each) and Jewels ($500 each) inside each Car as shown. Purses are placed face-down so nobody knows exactly how much they are worth. Put one Strongbox ($1000) and the Marshal pawn inside the Locomotive. Set the second Strongbox off to the side near the Locomotive — it might come into play later from a special event. Return any unused loot tokens to the box.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Bandit
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a Character. Take your Character card and place it face-up in front of you. Every bandit has a unique special ability printed on their card, so read yours carefully. You have got Ghost the sneaky one, Belle the untargetable beauty, Cheyenne the pickpocket, Tuco who shoots through roofs, Django whose bullets knock people back, and Doc the card-drawing genius. Pick whoever matches your outlaw style.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Your Bullets and Action Deck
walkthrough.text: Stack your six Bullet cards in numbered order to the left of your Character card. These represent your six-shooter — fully loaded and ready to cause trouble. Then take your 10 Action cards, shuffle them, and place them face-down to the right of your Character card. This is your personal draw deck. Every bandit has the same 10 Action cards: two Move, two Fire, two Robbery, two Punch, one Floor Change, and one Marshal.

walkthrough.title: Collect Your Starting Purse
walkthrough.text: Each player takes one $250 Purse token from the supply and places it face-down on their Character card. This is your starting loot — the pocket money you brought to the heist. Keep the value hidden from other players, though you can peek at your own Purses any time you want. Throughout the game, all loot you steal gets stacked on your Character card.

walkthrough.title: Build the Round Deck
walkthrough.text: Grab the Round cards that match your player count — use the 2-4 player set or the 5-6 player set. Randomly draw four of those seven Round cards and shuffle them together. Then pick one of the three Train Station cards at random. Stack the deck so the Train Station card is on the bottom. This gives you five rounds that tell the story of your train robbery, with a big finale at the station. Put any leftover Round cards back in the box. Set the 13 Neutral Bullet cards next to the Locomotive.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player and Place Bandits
walkthrough.text: Toss all the Bandit pawns for the Characters in play into a hat and draw one randomly. That player is the First Player for Round 1 and takes the Round card deck. Now place your Bandits on the train. Number the players starting with the First Player as Player 1, then clockwise. Odd-numbered players place their Bandit in the Caboose, which is the last Car. Even-numbered players place theirs in the Car next to the Caboose. Nobody starts on the roof. Saddle up — it is time to rob this train!

Set the 2nd Strongbox aside near the Locomotive

Add 1 random Train Station card on the bottom = 5 rounds total

Even players (2nd, 4th, 6th) start in the next Car forward
Nobody starts on a roof

walkthrough.title: The Goal: Be the Richest Bandit
walkthrough.text: Welcome to the Wild West, outlaw. Your goal is simple — end the game with more loot than anyone else. You will rob passengers, punch fellow bandits to steal their goods, and dodge the Marshal. After five rounds, everyone counts up the dollar value of their loot tokens. The bandit who shot the most bullets earns the Gunslinger title, worth a cool $1000 bonus. Richest bandit wins.

walkthrough.title: Round Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Each of the five rounds has two phases. Phase 1 is Schemin, where you play Action cards to plan your moves. Phase 2 is Stealin, where those plans actually happen. At the start of a round, shuffle your entire personal deck (Action cards plus any Bullet cards you have picked up) and draw six cards. The First Player flips the top Round card to reveal how this round plays out.

walkthrough.title: Phase 1: Schemin'
walkthrough.text: The Round card shows a row of icons indicating how many turns this phase has, usually three to five. Starting with the First Player and going clockwise, each player takes a turn. On your turn you either play one Action card face-up from your hand onto a shared central deck, or you skip playing a card and instead draw three cards from your personal deck. That is it — play a card or draw three. Once everyone has taken the indicated number of turns, Phase 1 ends and any unplayed cards go back on top of your personal deck.

walkthrough.title: Round Card Twists
walkthrough.text: The Round card can add spicy twists to certain turns. A Tunnel icon means cards played that turn are face-down instead of face-up, so nobody knows what you planned. A Speeding-Up icon means each player plays twice on that turn. A Switching icon reverses turn order to counter-clockwise for that turn. These twists keep you guessing about what your rivals are plotting. The Round card also shows an end-of-round Event that triggers after Phase 2.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: Stealin'
walkthrough.text: Now the fun begins. The First Player picks up the shared Action card deck and flips it over without changing the order. Cards are resolved one at a time from the top, which means the first card played in Schemin is the first action performed. When your card comes up, you must perform that action if possible. After resolving, you get your Action card back and place it on your personal deck. The key thing to remember: actions are mandatory. You planned it, now you have to do it, even if the situation has changed.

walkthrough.title: Action: Move
walkthrough.text: If your Bandit is inside the train, Move lets you go to one adjacent Car forward or backward. If you are on the roof, you can sprint up to three Cars in either direction. The Locomotive counts as a Car for movement. You must move — you cannot stay put. Rooftop running is faster because you are not squeezing past passengers and doors, but you are exposed to gunfire with clear lines of sight.

walkthrough.title: Action: Floor Change
walkthrough.text: This lets your Bandit climb from inside a Car up to its roof, or drop from the roof down into the Car below. It is the only way to switch levels. Going up to the roof gives you speed and helps you dodge the Marshal. Dropping inside lets you grab loot from the Car floor. Use this strategically to position yourself where the valuables are while staying out of danger.

walkthrough.title: Action: Fire
walkthrough.text: Time to use that six-shooter. When you Fire, you give one of your Bullet cards to a target Bandit. That Bullet goes onto their personal deck as a dead card, clogging up their future hands. If you are inside the train, you can only shoot into an adjacent Car interior. On the roof, you can shoot any Bandit on any other rooftop Car in your line of sight — meaning no other Bandit is between you and the target on the roof. You cannot shoot someone on the same spot as you. If you run out of Bullet cards, your Fire actions fizzle.

walkthrough.title: Action: Robbery
walkthrough.text: Grab one Loot token from the Car or rooftop where your Bandit currently stands and place it face-down on your Character card. Purses stay hidden, but Jewels ($500) and Strongboxes ($1000) are obviously valuable. If you are on the roof, you can only grab loot from the roof, not from inside the Car below. If there is no loot where you are, the Robbery has no effect. Timing your robberies around the Marshal and other bandits is key.

walkthrough.title: Action: Punch
walkthrough.text: Choose a Bandit on the exact same spot and floor as you. They drop one Loot token of your choice from their Character card onto the current Car floor. If it is a Purse, you cannot peek at the value. Then you shove the punched Bandit into an adjacent Car on the same floor — your choice of direction, except from the Locomotive you push backward and from the Caboose you push forward. Punching is how you steal the big prizes from rivals and mess up their plans.

walkthrough.title: Action: Marshal
walkthrough.text: Move the Marshal pawn one Car in either direction inside the train. The Marshal never climbs to the roof. When the Marshal enters a Car with Bandits inside, or when a Bandit enters the Marshal's Car, those Bandits immediately flee to the roof and each receives a Neutral Bullet card on their deck. The Marshal protects the Strongbox in the Locomotive, so luring him away is essential if you want that $1000 prize.

walkthrough.title: End of Round and Game End
walkthrough.text: After all Stealin actions resolve, check the Round card for an end-of-round Event. Events can move bandits, dish out Neutral Bullets, spawn a second Strongbox, or let lone bandits pickpocket extra Purses. After the Event, the player to the left of the current First Player becomes the new First Player and takes the remaining Round cards. After all five rounds, count up your loot. The player who fired the most Bullets (fewest remaining) wins the $1000 Gunslinger bonus — ties mean everyone tied gets $1000. Richest bandit wins. Tiebreaker goes to whoever received the fewest Bullet cards during the game.

summary.title: The Goal: Be the Richest Bandit
bullets: End the game with the most loot (dollar value of all tokens)
Gunslinger bonus: $1000 to whoever fired the most Bullets
Tiebreaker: fewest Bullet cards received

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Heist
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with three bandits — Doc, Cheyenne, and Ghost. Three players is the recommended count for learning Colt Express. We will show you how Schemin and Stealin work, demonstrate key actions, and highlight three common mistakes new outlaws make. Even if you have a different number of players, follow along to see the game in action. Ready to rob this train?

walkthrough.title: The Train Is Set
walkthrough.text: We have a three-Car train plus the Locomotive. The Caboose has two Purse tokens inside. The middle Car has a Purse and a Jewel. The front Car has a Purse. The Locomotive has the Marshal and one Strongbox. Doc is Player 1 (First Player), starting in the Caboose. Cheyenne is Player 2, starting in the middle Car. Ghost is Player 3, starting in the Caboose with Doc.

walkthrough.title: Draw Your Hands
walkthrough.text: Everyone shuffles their personal deck and draws six cards. Doc draws seven thanks to his special ability — he is the smartest bandit in the West and always gets an extra card. Look at your hand. You will see a mix of Move, Fire, Robbery, Punch, Floor Change, and Marshal cards. This is the ammunition for your schemin phase. Plan ahead — the order you play cards matters.

walkthrough.title: Round Card Revealed
walkthrough.text: Doc flips the Round card. It shows four turn icons: a normal turn, a Tunnel turn, a Speeding-Up turn, and a normal turn. That means this round has four turns of Schemin, but the second turn is hidden and the third turn everyone plays twice. There is also an end-of-round Event: Angry Marshal. Keep that in mind — the Marshal will be shooting at rooftop bandits at the end of this round.

walkthrough.title: Schemin' Turn 1: Doc Moves
walkthrough.text: Doc goes first. He plays a Move card face-up onto the center of the table, starting the shared Action deck. Doc is inside the Caboose and plans to move forward to grab loot. Next up is Cheyenne. She plays a Robbery card face-up on top of Doc's card — she is already inside the middle Car with a Jewel, so she plans to snag it. Ghost decides to draw three cards instead of playing, stocking up his hand for later. Smart — Ghost wants options.

walkthrough.title: Schemin' Turn 2: The Tunnel
walkthrough.text: This turn has the Tunnel icon, so all cards are played face-down. Nobody can see what anyone else is planning. Doc plays a card face-down. Cheyenne plays face-down. Ghost plays face-down. This is where the mind games happen. You know what you played, but you have no idea what your rivals did. Maybe Doc is moving toward you. Maybe Ghost is about to punch someone. The suspense is half the fun.

walkthrough.title: Mistake 1: Forgetting Tunnel Cards Are Hidden
walkthrough.text: Here is a classic rookie mistake. New players sometimes play their Tunnel card face-up out of habit, accidentally revealing their plan. In Colt Express, information is power. If everyone knows you played a Robbery card, they know you are going for loot and can plan to punch you or move to block you. Always double-check the Round card icons before playing. When you see the Tunnel symbol, play face-down. Keep your opponents guessing.

walkthrough.title: Schemin' Turn 3: Speeding Up
walkthrough.text: This turn has the Speeding-Up icon, meaning every player plays two cards (or draws three cards and plays one, or plays one and draws three, or draws six). Doc plays Fire and then Move. Cheyenne plays Move and Floor Change. Ghost plays Punch and Robbery. That is a lot of actions piling onto the shared deck. The Stealin phase is going to be wild.

walkthrough.title: Schemin' Turn 4: Final Plans
walkthrough.text: Last turn of Schemin. Doc plays a Robbery card. Cheyenne plays a Punch card. Ghost draws three cards, saving them for next round. With that, Phase 1 is complete. Any cards left in hand go back on top of your personal deck. The shared Action deck in the center is now a stack of everyone's plans, in the exact order they were played. Time for the fun part.

walkthrough.title: Stealin' Phase Begins
walkthrough.text: Doc picks up the shared deck and flips it over. The first card played during Schemin is now on top. We resolve them one by one. First up: Doc's Move card. Doc moves from the Caboose forward into the middle Car. He is now in the same Car as Cheyenne. Next card: Cheyenne's Robbery. She takes the Jewel from the middle Car floor and places it on her Character card. That is $500 in the bag.

walkthrough.title: Tunnel Cards Revealed
walkthrough.text: Now we get to the Tunnel cards. They were played face-down, but during Stealin they flip face-up and resolve normally. Doc's hidden card was a Robbery — he grabs a Purse from the middle Car. Cheyenne's was a Floor Change — she climbs to the roof. Ghost's was a Move — he heads forward from the Caboose into the middle Car. The hidden cards add an element of surprise, but they resolve just like any other action.

walkthrough.title: Mistake 2: Poor Planning During Schemin'
walkthrough.text: Here is where things go sideways for Ghost. During Schemin, Ghost played Punch after Move. The problem? By the time Ghost's Punch resolves, Doc has already moved away thanks to actions earlier in the stack. Ghost is now in the middle Car with nobody to punch. The Punch fizzles because there is no valid target. The lesson: when you plan your actions during Schemin, think about what other players are likely to do before your cards resolve. The order of the entire stack matters, not just your own cards.

walkthrough.title: The Action Stack Continues
walkthrough.text: Continuing through the Speeding-Up cards: Doc's Fire targets Ghost in the middle Car — Ghost receives one of Doc's Bullet cards on his deck. That is a dead card Ghost will draw in future rounds. Doc's second Speeding-Up card was Move, so Doc advances to the front Car. Cheyenne's Move brings her across the rooftops. Her Floor Change drops her into the front Car. Ghost's Robbery grabs the remaining Purse from the middle Car floor.

walkthrough.title: Final Actions Resolve
walkthrough.text: Last cards in the stack: Doc's Robbery lets him grab a Purse from the front Car. Cheyenne's Punch targets Doc — she knocks a Purse off Doc's Character card onto the front Car floor and shoves Doc backward into the middle Car. Cheyenne is a pickpocket, so her special ability lets her take the dropped Purse. Beautiful play by Cheyenne. The Stealin phase is done.

walkthrough.title: Mistake 3: Ignoring the Marshal
walkthrough.text: Notice that nobody played a Marshal card this entire round. That means the Marshal sat in the Locomotive the whole time, guarding the $1000 Strongbox. New players often forget about the Marshal action entirely, treating it as useless. But moving the Marshal into a Car full of rival bandits sends them scrambling to the roof and gives them Neutral Bullet cards. It is one of the most powerful disruption tools in the game. Conversely, moving the Marshal away from the Locomotive is the only way to safely grab that Strongbox. Do not sleep on the Marshal card.

walkthrough.title: End-of-Round Event: Angry Marshal
walkthrough.text: This round's Event is Angry Marshal. The Marshal shoots at every Bandit on the roof of his Car — they each receive a Neutral Bullet card. Then the Marshal moves one Car toward the Caboose. In our example, no one was on the Locomotive roof, so no one gets shot. But the Marshal just moved into the front Car. Any bandit who was inside the front Car would have been forced to the roof and taken a Neutral Bullet. Events like this can completely change the board state, so always check the Round card Event before you start Schemin.

walkthrough.title: Passing the Hat
walkthrough.text: Round 1 is over. Cheyenne (the player to Doc's left) becomes the new First Player and takes the remaining Round card deck. Everyone shuffles their entire personal deck — that includes your 10 Action cards plus any Bullet cards you have collected. Draw six fresh cards (seven for Doc). Flip the next Round card and start Schemin again. Four more rounds to go.

walkthrough.title: Round 1 Scoreboard
walkthrough.text: Let us check the haul after Round 1. Cheyenne snagged a Jewel ($500) and pickpocketed a Purse from Doc — solid round. Doc grabbed two Purses minus one that Cheyenne stole, so he has his starting Purse plus one new one. Ghost got one Purse but also took a Bullet card from Doc, which will clog his hand next round. Nobody touched the $1000 Strongbox. Four rounds remain to change the leaderboard.

walkthrough.title: Character Abilities Recap
walkthrough.text: Let us recap what the special abilities did this round. Doc drew seven cards instead of six, giving him more options during Schemin. Cheyenne's pickpocket ability let her keep the Purse she punched off Doc instead of leaving it on the floor. Ghost could have played his first turn card face-down (his special ability), but he chose to draw three cards instead and missed his chance. Each ability shapes how you play, so lean into your Character's strengths.

walkthrough.title: Key Takeaways
walkthrough.text: Three big lessons from this practice round. First, always check the Round card for Tunnel, Speeding-Up, and Switching icons before you start playing cards. Second, think about the full action stack during Schemin — your card might resolve after everyone else has moved, so plan accordingly. Third, the Marshal is a weapon. Use him to disrupt opponents and clear a path to the Strongbox. Keep these in mind and you will outscheme any posse.

walkthrough.title: Playing With More Bandits
walkthrough.text: With four to six players, the train gets longer, there is more loot on the board, and the action stack gets crazier. More bandits means more chaos during Stealin — plans go off the rails more often, and that is the whole fun. The 5-6 player Round cards have more turns and wilder events. Adjust your strategy: with more opponents, punching and shooting become more valuable since there are more targets and more loot to knock loose.

walkthrough.title: Before You Ride Off
walkthrough.text:
You have seen the full cycle of Schemin and Stealin, watched three bandits duke it out, and learned from three common mistakes. Remember: check the Round card before playing, think about action order, and never forget about the Marshal. You are ready to rob the Union Pacific Express on your own.

First — check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second — reference the Appendix for a quick guide to all actions, characters, loot, and events.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Yeehaw!

summary.title: Before You Ride Off
bullets: You've seen full Schemin' and Stealin' cycle with 3 common mistakes
Check General Tips for strategy, Appendix for quick reference
Ask the GM Guide anytime via Q&A tab — yeehaw!

walkthrough.title: Read the Round Card Before You Scheme
walkthrough.text: Before you play a single card, study the Round card. Count the turns, spot the Tunnel and Speeding-Up icons, and read the end-of-round Event. The Event is especially important — if it is Angry Marshal, you probably do not want to end the round on a roof. If it is Pickpocketing, try to be alone in a Car with Purses. The Round card is your crystal ball. Use it.

walkthrough.title: Think in Sequences, Not Single Cards
walkthrough.text: New players tend to play one card at a time without thinking about the order. But Colt Express is all about sequences. Move then Rob is very different from Rob then Move. Fire then Move gets you out of retaliation range. Plan two or three cards ahead during Schemin, and try to predict what opponents will do between your cards. The best outlaws think about the whole stack.

walkthrough.title: Drawing Cards Is Not Wasting a Turn
walkthrough.text: Many new players feel guilty about drawing three cards instead of playing one. Do not. Drawing cards fills your hand with options for Speeding-Up turns and future rounds. It is especially smart on Tunnel turns when you cannot see what others are doing anyway. A well-timed draw sets you up to dominate the next few turns.

walkthrough.title: Shoot Early, Shoot Often
walkthrough.text: Firing Bullet cards at opponents is not just about the Gunslinger bonus. Every Bullet you land is a dead card in their deck, meaning they draw fewer useful Action cards in future rounds. Shooting someone early in the game hurts them for the rest of it. And if you win the Gunslinger title, that is a free $1000 — the same as a Strongbox. Do not hoard your Bullets.

walkthrough.title: The Roof Is Your Highway
walkthrough.text: Moving inside the train is painfully slow — one Car at a time. On the roof, you can sprint up to three Cars. If you need to get somewhere fast, climb up, run across, and drop back down. Yes, you are exposed to long-range gunfire on the roof, but the speed advantage is worth it. Just be careful about the Angry Marshal event.

walkthrough.title: Use the Marshal as a Weapon
walkthrough.text: The Marshal action is one of the most underrated cards in the game. Moving the Marshal into a crowded Car sends everyone to the roof and gives them all Neutral Bullet cards. That is mass disruption in one action. Alternatively, pulling the Marshal away from the Locomotive opens the path to the $1000 Strongbox. Think of the Marshal card as a tactical nuke, not a throwaway.

walkthrough.title: Punching Wins Games
walkthrough.text: Punching is the most powerful action in Colt Express. You choose which loot your opponent drops, you choose which direction they get shoved, and if you are playing Cheyenne, you keep the dropped Purse. Punch rivals who just grabbed a Strongbox or Jewel. Even without Cheyenne's ability, dropping loot onto the floor means you or another bandit can pick it up later.

walkthrough.title: Lean Into Your Character
walkthrough.text: Every Character ability is genuinely strong if you play to it. Ghost should always play face-down on turn one for information advantage. Belle should stay near other bandits so she cannot be targeted. Cheyenne should prioritize punching over robbing to trigger her pickpocket bonus. Tuco should climb to roofs for vertical shots. Django should shoot opponents toward the Marshal. Doc should take advantage of his extra card to play more aggressively. Your ability is your edge — use it every round.

summary.title: Read the Round Card Before You Scheme
bullets: Study turn icons: Tunnel, Speeding-Up, Switching
Check the end-of-round Event and plan accordingly
Angry Marshal = stay off roofs; Pickpocketing = be alone with Purses

summary.title: Think in Sequences, Not Single Cards
bullets: Card order matters — Move-then-Rob differs from Rob-then-Move
Plan 2-3 cards ahead during Schemin'
Predict opponent actions that resolve between your cards

summary.title: Drawing Cards Is Not Wasting a Turn
bullets: Drawing 3 cards fills your hand for Speeding-Up turns and future rounds
Especially smart on Tunnel turns when you can't see others' plays
A stocked hand gives you flexibility

summary.title: Shoot Early, Shoot Often
bullets: Each Bullet clogs the target's deck permanently
Shooting early compounds the disadvantage over remaining rounds
Gunslinger bonus = $1000 (same as a Strongbox)

summary.title: The Roof Is Your Highway
bullets: Roof movement: up to 3 Cars vs. 1 Car inside
Climb up, sprint, drop down to reach distant loot quickly
Watch out for Angry Marshal event and rooftop shooters

summary.title: Use the Marshal as a Weapon
bullets: Move Marshal into crowded Car = everyone flees to roof + Neutral Bullets
Move Marshal away from Locomotive = path to $1000 Strongbox
One of the most powerful disruption tools in the game

summary.title: Punching Wins Games
bullets: You choose which loot the target drops
You choose which direction to shove them
Cheyenne keeps dropped Purses; others can rob dropped loot later

summary.title: Lean Into Your Character
bullets: Ghost: always play face-down on turn 1 for stealth
Belle: stay near others so she can't be targeted
Cheyenne: punch to pickpocket; Tuco: climb for vertical shots
Django: shoot toward Marshal; Doc: play aggressively with extra card

walkthrough.title: Count the Bullets
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to how many Bullets each player has fired. If someone has already spent four or five of their six Bullets, they cannot threaten you with Fire anymore. Conversely, if you have only fired one or two, you have plenty of ammo and rivals should fear you. Bullet counting also tells you who is in the running for Gunslinger. If you are close to the lead, spending your remaining Bullets becomes even more valuable since that $1000 bonus is one of the biggest payoffs in the game.

walkthrough.title: Track the Loot
walkthrough.text: Purse values are hidden, but Jewels and Strongboxes are visible. Keep a mental tally of who grabbed what. If someone snagged the Strongbox, they are instantly the biggest target. If you punched someone and their dropped Purse is sitting on the Car floor, remember where it is. Tracking visible loot helps you decide who to punch and where to move. The richest bandit is always the biggest threat.

walkthrough.title: Control the Locomotive
walkthrough.text: The Locomotive Strongbox is worth $1000, making it the single most valuable piece of loot on the train. The winning strategy often involves luring the Marshal out of the Locomotive so you can drop in and rob it. Play a Marshal card to push him backward, then Move into the Locomotive and Rob on your next turn. Be warned: everyone else is thinking the same thing. Timing the Locomotive grab is the chess match at the heart of Colt Express.

walkthrough.title: Exploit the Speeding-Up Turn
walkthrough.text: The Speeding-Up turn is your chance to execute devastating two-card combos. Move-then-Rob lets you close distance and grab loot in one turn. Punch-then-Robbery lets you knock loot off someone and immediately pick it up. Floor Change-then-Move lets you escape to the roof and sprint away. Plan your hand specifically around Speeding-Up turns — they are where big plays happen.

walkthrough.title: Use Tunnel Turns for High-Risk Plays
walkthrough.text: Tunnel turns are face-down, so no one sees your card. This is the perfect time to play a Marshal card to surprise everyone, or a Move card to reposition without tipping off your next robbery. Experienced players save their most disruptive or deceptive cards for Tunnel turns. If you play a Marshal card during a visible turn, everyone sees it coming and plans around it. Play it face-down, and chaos ensues.

walkthrough.title: Position for Events
walkthrough.text: The end-of-round Event should shape your entire round strategy. If the event is Braking (all rooftop bandits slide one Car toward the Locomotive), plan your roof position so the slide puts you where you want to be. If it is Pickpocketing (lone bandits grab a Purse), try to end the round alone in a Car with Purses. If it is Hostage-Taking (bandits in/on the Locomotive get $250), position near the Locomotive. Events are free loot or free damage — make sure you are on the right side of them.

walkthrough.title: Django's Displacement Chains
walkthrough.text: If you are playing Django, or playing against him, understand displacement chains. When Django shoots someone, the target moves one Car in the direction of the shot. This can push a bandit into the Marshal, triggering a forced climb to the roof and a Neutral Bullet. It can also push someone off a favorable position or into a dead-end at the Caboose. Against Django, avoid standing in a straight line between him and the Marshal. As Django, line up shots that push rivals into the worst possible spots.

walkthrough.title: Cheyenne's Punch Economy
walkthrough.text: Cheyenne turns every Punch into a double action: knock loot off the target and pocket the Purse. This means Cheyenne should prioritize Punch cards over Robbery cards whenever possible. A Punch that displaces an opponent and steals their $500 Purse is strictly better than a simple Robbery. Cheyenne players should aggressively position near loot-heavy bandits and chain Move-Punch combos during Speeding-Up turns.

walkthrough.title: Ghost's Information Advantage
walkthrough.text: Ghost can play the first card of each round face-down, even on non-Tunnel turns. This is more powerful than it sounds. When everyone else plays face-up on turn 1, you see all their plans while hiding yours. Use this to play Marshal or Move cards that catch opponents flat-footed. The key mistake Ghost players make is drawing three cards on turn 1 instead of playing face-down — once you draw, you lose the ability for that round. Always consider using Ghost's ability before deciding to draw.

walkthrough.title: Late-Game Strongbox Rush
walkthrough.text: In the final round, the Train Station Event changes everything. If the second Strongbox has entered play via Take It All, there may be two $1000 prizes on the board. In the last round, be aggressive. Fire Bullets to lock down the Gunslinger title, punch the leading player to redistribute their loot, and make a final dash for any unclaimed high-value tokens. Conservative play in Round 5 loses games. This is a train robbery — go out with a bang.

bullets: Track how many Bullets each player has remaining
Players with few Bullets left can't threaten with Fire
Gunslinger race ($1000) makes spending Bullets extra valuable

summary.title: Track the Loot
bullets: Purse values hidden, but Jewels and Strongboxes are visible
Remember where dropped loot lands on the floor
Target the richest bandit with punches

summary.title: Control the Locomotive
bullets: Locomotive Strongbox = $1000, biggest prize on the train
Lure Marshal out with Marshal card, then Move in and Rob
Timing the grab is the core chess match of the game

summary.title: Exploit the Speeding-Up Turn
bullets: Two-card combos: Move+Rob, Punch+Robbery, Floor Change+Move
Plan your hand around Speeding-Up turns
Biggest plays happen when you get two consecutive actions

summary.title: Use Tunnel Turns for High-Risk Plays
bullets: Save Marshal or surprise Move cards for Tunnel turns
Face-down play hides your disruption from opponents
Marshal during a Tunnel = maximum surprise

summary.title: Position for Events
bullets: Braking: plan roof position so slide moves you advantageously
Pickpocketing: end round alone in a Car with Purses
Hostage-Taking: be in/on Locomotive for free $250

summary.title: Django's Displacement Chains
bullets: Django's Fire pushes target 1 Car in direction of shot
Can push rivals into the Marshal (forced roof + Neutral Bullet)
Against Django: avoid standing between him and the Marshal

summary.title: Cheyenne's Punch Economy
bullets: Cheyenne keeps Purses knocked off by punching — double value
Prioritize Punch over Robbery when possible
Chain Move+Punch during Speeding-Up for maximum pickpocketing

summary.title: Ghost's Information Advantage
bullets: First card each round can be face-down even on non-Tunnel turns
See everyone else's face-up plays while hiding yours
Never draw on turn 1 without considering using Ghost's ability first

summary.title: Late-Game Strongbox Rush
bullets: Round 5: be aggressive — fire remaining Bullets, punch the leader
Take It All event may add a 2nd $1000 Strongbox
Conservative play in the final round loses games

entries.definition: Move your Bandit inside the train to 1 adjacent Car, or on the roof up to 3 Cars, forward or backward. You must move — staying put is not allowed.
Switch your Bandit between the interior and the roof of the same Car. The only way to change levels.
Give 1 of your Bullet cards to a target Bandit. Inside: target adjacent Car interior. Roof: any other rooftop Bandit in line of sight. Cannot target your own spot.
Take 1 Loot token from your current spot (same floor) and place it face-down on your Character card. No loot = no effect.
Target a Bandit on your exact spot and floor. They drop 1 Loot token (your choice) onto the floor, then you shove them to an adjacent Car on the same floor.
Move the Marshal pawn 1 Car inside the train in either direction. Any Bandits in the Marshal's new Car flee to the roof and receive a Neutral Bullet card each.
On the roof, you can shoot any Bandit on a different Car's roof as long as no other Bandit is between you and the target. Bandits on the same Car are side by side, not blocking each other.
A dead card given to Bandits who encounter the Marshal or are hit by certain Events. Placed on your personal deck, it clogs your hand with useless draws.
Loot token worth $250 to $500. Value is hidden — placed and kept face-down. You can peek at your own Purses but not your opponents'.
Loot token worth $500. Six Jewels total in the game. Value is not hidden.
Loot token worth $1000. Two in the game — one starts in the Locomotive, the second may enter play via the Take It All event.
At game end, the player who fired the most Bullets (fewest remaining) wins $1000. If tied, all tied players receive $1000 each.
Phase 1 of each round. Players take turns playing Action cards face-up (usually) to a shared central deck, or drawing 3 cards from their personal deck.
Phase 2 of each round. The shared Action deck is flipped and resolved card by card from the top. Actions are mandatory if possible.
Special ability: On the first turn of each round, Ghost may play his Action card face-down even if it is not a Tunnel turn. If he draws instead, the ability is lost for that round.
Special ability: Belle cannot be the target of Fire or Punch if another Bandit can be targeted instead. She is effectively shielded by nearby bandits.
Special ability: When Cheyenne punches a Bandit, she keeps the dropped Purse (if it is a Purse). Jewels and Strongboxes still drop to the floor normally.
Special ability: Tuco can shoot a Bandit on the same Car but on the other floor (through the roof). His shots are not limited to his own level.
Special ability: When Django shoots a Bandit, the target is pushed 1 Car in the direction of the shot. This can push rivals into the Marshal.
Special ability: At the beginning of each round, Doc draws 7 cards instead of 6. The extra card gives him more options during Schemin'.
During a Tunnel turn, all Action cards are played face-down instead of face-up. Nobody can see what others planned until Stealin'.
During a Speeding-Up turn, each player plays twice: draw 3 cards and/or play cards in any combination of two sub-actions.
During a Switching turn, play order reverses to counter-clockwise, starting from the First Player.
The Marshal shoots Bandits on the roof of his Car (they get Neutral Bullet cards). Then the Marshal moves 1 Car toward the Caboose.
Each Bandit who is alone on their spot can take 1 Purse token from that spot if any are available.
Each Bandit on the roof above the Marshal loses their least valuable Purse. No Purse = no loss (Jewels and Strongboxes are safe).
All Bandits on roofs move to the roof of the Caboose.
All Bandits on roofs slide 1 Car forward (toward the Locomotive).
Place the second Strongbox ($1000) inside the Car where the Marshal currently is.
All Bandits who are inside Cars (not on roofs) receive a Neutral Bullet card each.
Each Bandit in the Locomotive or on its roof receives $250 ransom.
The final Round card placed at the bottom of the Round deck. It determines the setup of Round 5 and the game's climactic event.
The last Car at the rear of the train. Odd-numbered players start here. Bandits punched in the Caboose can only be pushed forward.
The front Car of the train. Contains the Marshal and the first Strongbox at game start. Counts as a Car for all movement and action purposes.
Your face-down draw pile containing your 10 Action cards plus any Bullet cards received. Shuffled at the start of each round.
Optional rule adding a discard deck. Discard Bullet cards when drawn, keep desired hand cards between rounds, and reshuffle discards when the draw deck runs out. Gives players more control.
Each player controls a team of 2 Characters with a combined 11-card deck. Uses Expert Variant. No Gunslinger prize. Richest team wins.
